name: urban_development_communications_millhaven_jus_mer
premise: A regional manager wants an exception to policy; compliance says any carve-out must be written, time-limited, and auditable. In Urban Development, the situation tagged internally around urban development communications millhaven jus mer has converged with local pressure in the Millhaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Finance notes that the fastest mitigation spends down a restricted reserve that another program legally depends on next quarter. An internal red-team note and a customer ticket describe the same failure mode with incompatible timelines. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. No option cleanly satisfies both justice and mercy; the least-wrong path requires naming who absorbs downside if the bet is wrong.

agents.name: Omar Petrov
agents.identity: I am Omar Petrov, the incident lead whose name will appear on the after-action report in Urban Development. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat mercy as disposable collateral every time justice spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Sana Bennett
agents.identity: I am Sana Bennett, the field counterpart whose constituency will feel the decision first in Urban Development. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so justice cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes mercy in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will impose a narrow, time-boxed intervention with human override, subgroup checks, and a hard stop if harm rises—accepting slower relief to protect mercy.
I will authorize a broad immediate action to stabilize outcomes now, with post-hoc measurement and correction—accepting elevated risk to mercy to secure justice before the window closes.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next irreversible system or legal step is imminent. Omar Petrov must choose under justice versus mercy. What does Omar Petrov do?
